stem: A bank hires a security company to carry out penetration testing on its online banking system.
model_answer: Penetration testing is an authorised, controlled attempt to attack a system in the same way a real attacker would. The aim is to find vulnerabilities (weak passwords, unpatched software, misconfigured servers, flawed input validation, etc.) before a criminal does, so they can be fixed. The results are reported back to the organisation and used to improve security; it does not aim to damage the live system.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain what penetration testing is and why the bank has chosen to do it.

mark_points.text: an authorised/controlled simulated attack on the system
accepted_alternatives: testers act like an attacker with permission

mark_points.text: aims to identify vulnerabilities/weaknesses in the system
so the organisation can fix them before a real attacker exploits them
accepted_alternatives: weaknesses can be patched before criminals find them
